title: ClioRing application settings
description: Local ClioRing desktop settings. This file controls Ring presentation, workspace discovery, and the optional development clio MCP child.

$schema.title: Schema reference
$schema.description: Relative path or URL to this JSON Schema for editor validation and hover help.

WorkspaceFolder.title: Workspace folder
WorkspaceFolder.description: Absolute folder scanned for clio workspaces.

Hotkey.title: Global hotkey
Hotkey.description: Optional global gesture used to summon Ring. An absent, blank, or invalid value uses the application default.

LogsFolder.title: Logs folder
LogsFolder.description: Optional absolute folder for logs and deployment receipts. Blank or absent defaults to the Logs folder beside clio-ring.exe.

Channel.title: Ring channel label
Channel.description: Display/deployment label shown in the Ring build badge, such as release, dev, or preview. This value does not select the clio executable or MCP server.

ClioRuntimeMode.title: Active clio runtime
ClioRuntimeMode.description: Selects the clio child used on the next Ring launch. release starts the installed clio dotnet tool from PATH. development uses the saved DevClioPath or ClioIpc target without deleting it.

DevClioPath.title: Development clio override
DevClioPath.description: Optional absolute path to a trusted local development clio.dll or clio.exe. Ring executes it with your user privileges. In development mode, a valid value takes precedence over ClioIpc. Switching to release preserves this target.

experiments.title: Experimental features
experiments.description: Feature switches that remain off unless explicitly enabled.

ClioIpc.title: Enable clio IPC
ClioIpc.description: Enables the experimental MCP-over-stdio Clio IPC surface. This must be true to expose the IPC proof UI.

clioIpc.title: Explicit clio MCP launch
clioIpc.description: Explicit trusted development child-process command used in development mode when DevClioPath is absent or invalid. Ring executes it with your user privileges. Switching to release preserves this target.

Command.title: Executable
Command.description: Executable used to launch the clio MCP child, for example dotnet or clio.

Args.title: Arguments
Args.description: Arguments passed to Command. For a framework-dependent build, pass the clio.dll path followed by mcp-server.

WorkingDirectory.title: Working directory
WorkingDirectory.description: Optional working directory for the clio child. Blank or absent uses the Ring launch directory.
